name: add-tracing
tagline: Guides adding OpenTelemetry tracing spans to Metabase's Clojure backend per module and naming conventions.
verdict: Consistent, highly actionable internal dev skill with clear conventions and SQL-sanitization guidance to avoid data leaks. Gaps: no version/changelog signal, no failure handling if lint/tests actually fail, no exclusion scope (risks misfiring vs the sibling analytics-events skill), and clj-kondo/clojure tooling used with no requirement or availability check.

highlights.summary: No NOT-for/exclusion guidance anywhere; plausible confusion with sibling 'analytics-events' skill (tracking vs tracing).
States expected zero-error lint/test outcome but never says what to do if clj-kondo or tests actually fail.
`clj-kondo --lint` and `clojure -X:dev:test` invoked with no requirements section or availability check.
